title: Fastly API tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don't need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Fastly API Token detected in variable assignment (hex format). Fastly API tokens provide access to manage CDN configurations, purge cached content, deploy Compute@Edge services, and control traffic routing globally. Exposing this token allows attackers to purge or corrupt your CDN cache, deploy malicious edge code, modify routing rules to redirect traffic, and access service configuration data. Revoke immediately at manage.fastly.com/account/personal/tokens.

Fastly API Token detected in variable assignment (alphanumeric format). Fastly tokens control CDN infrastructure including cache management, WAF rules, TLS certificates, and edge compute deployments. Unauthorized access can disrupt service delivery, expose cached sensitive data, and compromise security rules for all traffic flowing through your Fastly service. Revoke immediately at manage.fastly.com.

Fastly API token detected in Fastly-Key HTTP header. The Fastly-Key header is used to authenticate API requests to Fastly's management API. Exposing this header value allows attackers to make API calls to manage your CDN configuration, purge caches, and deploy edge services. Remove from code and revoke at manage.fastly.com.
